text: Statistical heterogeneity remains a fundamental challenge in federated learning (FL), often degrading the generalization performance of global models across diverse client populations. To mitigate this, we introduce Federated Learning with Adaptive Local Aggregation (FedALA), a novel framework designed to intelligently extract relevant information from the global model to enhance personalized local models. At the core of our approach is the Adaptive Local Aggregation (ALA) module. Before each local training phase, the ALA module adaptively merges the downloaded global parameters with the existing local state, tailoring the initialization to better align with the specific objectives of each client. We conducted comprehensive experiments across five benchmark datasets in both computer vision and natural language processing. The results demonstrate that FedALA consistently surpasses eleven state-of-the-art baselines, improving test accuracy by up to 3.27%. Additionally, we show that the ALA module is highly versatile; when integrated into other popular FL frameworks, it yields substantial performance gains, increasing test accuracy by as much as 24.19%. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/TsingZ0/FedALA.
